articles.title: UK Police Seek To Widen Ex-Prince Andrew Probe, Including Sexual Misconduct Allegations
articles.content:
伦敦（美联社）——英国警方周五呼吁目击者提供线索，以扩大对前安德鲁王子涉嫌不当行为的调查，包括性行为不端。

泰晤士河谷警察局在更新对前王子安德鲁·蒙巴顿-温莎（现称）的公职人员失职指控调查时发表了声明。该警局负责伦敦以西地区，而安德鲁王子多年居住的区域就在这一带。

安德鲁于2月19日被捕，并被警方问话数小时，调查与他和已故定罪性罪犯杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的友谊相关的指控。在英国，这一逮捕行动曾是一项非同寻常的举措，因为当局过去一直试图保护王室免受丑闻影响。

安德鲁多次否认有任何不当行为。

此前，警方表示正在“评估”有关安德鲁在2010年担任英国国际贸易特别代表期间向爱泼斯坦发送贸易报告的指控。这些报告内容源于两人之间的通信，而这些通信在美方司法部公布其对爱泼斯坦调查中获得的数百万页文件后才得以公开。

但据英国新闻社报道，调查人员再次呼吁公众提供线索，因为担心公众误以为警方只在调查与贸易报告相关的指控，而实际上“公职人员失职”可能涵盖更广泛的违法行为。

“公职人员失职”是一项可以表现为多种形式的罪名，使得调查变得复杂，助理总警长奥利弗·赖特表示。“我们经验丰富的侦探团队正在仔细审查公众和其他来源提供的大量信息。”

“公职人员失职”可以包括泄露机密财务信息、财务不当行为、故意失职、性行为不当等。它还涵盖腐败和不当干预、利益冲突、妨碍司法公正、不诚实或欺诈行为，或导致个人利益的不当行为。

警方表示，在美方文件公布后，他们正在调查“多项涉嫌不当行为”的情况，并正与美国司法部合作，以获取与调查相关的更多信息。

泰晤士河谷警方还更新了对2010年一名女子被带到温莎进行性行为的调查情况。

警方在声明中表示：“如果她希望向警方举报此事，警方将认真对待，并以谨慎、敏感和尊重隐私的方式处理。”

佛罗里达州律师布拉德·爱德华兹1月告诉英国广播公司，他代表一名女子称，爱泼斯坦在2010年将她送往英国，与安德鲁在温莎的家中发生性关系。

这些文件显示了这位富有的金融家如何利用其国际网络中富有且有影响力的朋友们来获取影响力，并性剥削年轻女性和女孩。

文件的发布对英国产生了强烈影响，这一丑闻引发了关于社会上层人士（如贵族、高级政客和有影响力的商人）如何行使权力的质疑。

英国九个警察局表示，他们正在调查与爱泼斯坦相关的潜在不当行为。

英国前驻美大使彼得·曼德尔森去年因文件显示他与爱泼斯坦的关系比此前承认的更深而被解职。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING

LONDON (AP) — British police appealed for witnesses Friday as they sought to broaden their investigation into potential offenses by the former Prince Andrew, including sexual misconduct.

Thames Valley Police issued the statement as it updated reporters on its investigation into allegations of misconduct in public office by the former prince, now known as Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor. The force covers the area west of London where Mountbatten-Windsor lived for many years.

Advertisement

Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested on Feb. 19 and held for hours as police questioned him about the allegations, linked to his friendship with the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The arrest was an extraordinary move in a country where authorities once sought to shield the royal family from embarrassment.

Mountbatten-Windsor has repeatedly denied wrongdoing.

Police previously said they were “assessing” reports that Mountbatten-Windsor sent trade reports to Epstein in 2010 when the then-prince was Britain’s special envoy for international trade. Those reports were based on correspondence between the two men that became public when the U.S. Justice Department released millions of pages of documents from its investigation into Epstein.

Advertisement

But detectives renewed their appeal for witnesses amid concern that the public believes they are only investigating allegations linked to the trade reports, when in fact misconduct in public office can apply to a much broader list of offenses, Britain’s Press Association reported.

“Misconduct in public office is a crime that can take different forms, making this a complex investigation,’’ Assistant Chief Constable Oliver Wright said. “Our team of very experienced detectives are working meticulously through a significant amount of information that has come in from the public and other sources.”

Misconduct in public office can include sharing confidential financial information, financial misconduct, willful neglect of duty, and sexual misconduct. It also covers corruption and improper interference, conflict of interest, perverting the course of justice, dishonesty or fraudulent conduct or misconduct leading to personal gain.

Advertisement

Police said they are looking into “a number of aspects of alleged misconduct” following the release of the U.S. documents, and they are working with the Justice Department to get additional information related to the investigation.

Thames Valley Police also issued an update on its inquiry into allegations that a woman was taken to a location in Windsor for sexual purposes in 2010.

“Should she wish to report this to police it will be taken seriously and handled with care, sensitivity and respect for her privacy,’’ police said in a statement.

Advertisement

Florida attorney Brad Edwards told the BBC in January that he represented a woman who said that Epstein sent her to England in 2010 to have a sexual encounter with Mountbatten-Windsor at his home in Windsor.

King Charles III stripped his younger brother of his royal titles late last year as he sought to distance the royal family from the fallout from the Epstein scandal.

Those files showed how the wealthy financier used an international web of rich, powerful friends to gain influence and sexually exploit young women and girls.

Advertisement

The fallout from the document release has been strongly felt in the U.K., where the scandal has raised questions about the way power is wielded by the upper echelon of society such as the aristocracy, senior politicians and influential business owners.

Nine police forces in the U.K. have said they are investigating potential wrongdoing related to Epstein.

Britain’s former ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson was fired last year after documents revealed that he had a longer and deeper relationship with Epstein than he previously acknowledged.

Advertisement

Related

jeffrey epstein

Andrew Mountbatten Windsor

Documents Show Queen Elizabeth Was Eager For Ex-Prince Andrew To Become Trade Envoy
Norway's Crown Princess Tearfully Breaks Her Silence On Her Ties To Jeffrey Epstein
Epstein Survivor Praises Prince Andrew Arrest: 'We Are Doing Nothing In The United States'
British Royals Draw Further Back From Andrew As More Allegations Against Ex-Prince Emerge

articles.title: Supreme Leader Says Enriched Uranium Must Stay In Iran, Iranian Sources Say
articles.content:
迪拜5月21日（路透社）——两名伊朗高级消息人士称，伊朗最高领袖已发布指令，要求该国接近武器级水平的浓缩铀不得运往国外，这使德黑兰在美国和平谈判中的一项主要要求上立场更为强硬。

哈梅内伊大阿亚图拉的命令可能进一步令美国总统特朗普感到沮丧，并使美以对伊战争结束谈判复杂化。

特朗普周四誓言，美国不会允许伊朗拥有大量高浓缩铀库存。

“我们会得到它。我们不需要它，也不想要它。我们得到后可能会销毁它，但我们不会让他们拥有它，”特朗普在白宫对记者说。

以色列官员告诉路透社，特朗普已向以色列保证，伊朗制造核武器所需的大量高浓缩铀库存将被运出伊朗，任何和平协议都必须包含相关条款。

以色列、美国和其他西方国家长期指责伊朗寻求核武器，包括指出伊朗将铀浓缩至60%的水平，远高于民用所需，接近制造核武器所需的90%。伊朗否认寻求核武器。

以色列总理内塔尼亚胡表示，在伊朗境内浓缩铀被清除、德黑兰终止对代理民兵的支持、以及其弹道导弹能力被消除之前，他不会认为战争已经结束。

两名伊朗消息人士之一因事关机密而匿名表示：“最高领袖的指令，以及体制内的共识是，浓缩铀库存不应离开该国。”

消息人士称，伊朗高级官员认为，将这些材料运往国外将使该国在面对美国和以色列未来攻击时更加脆弱。哈梅内伊对最重要国家事务拥有最终决定权。

当被问及对此报道置评时，白宫发言人奥利维亚·韦尔斯表示：“特朗普总统已明确表示美国的红线，并将只达成优先考虑美国人民的协议。”

伊朗外交部未回应置评请求。

伊朗高级官员间的深度不信任

美以于2月28日对伊朗发动空袭后，战争暂时停火，但伊朗向驻有美军的海湾国家发射了导弹，以色列与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党之间也爆发了战斗。

但和平努力尚未取得重大突破，美国对伊朗港口的封锁以及德黑兰对全球石油供应要道霍尔木兹海峡的控制使巴基斯坦调解的谈判更加复杂。

两名伊朗高级消息人士表示，伊朗国内对美国暂停敌对行动存在深刻怀疑，认为这是华盛顿制造安全假象后重新发动空袭的战术欺骗。

伊朗最高和平谈判代表卡利巴夫周三表示，“敌人的明显和隐蔽动作”表明美国人正在准备新的攻击。

特朗普周三表示，如果伊朗不同意和平协议，美国已准备好对德黑兰发动进一步攻击，但他暗示华盛顿可以等待几天以“获得正确答案”。

消息人士称，双方已开始缩小一些分歧，但在德黑兰的核计划问题上仍存在更深层次的分歧——包括浓缩铀库存的命运，以及德黑兰要求承认其浓缩铀的权利。

伊朗在对浓缩铀库存问题上立场更趋强硬

伊朗官员多次表示，德黑兰的首要任务是确保战争永久结束，并获得美国和以色列不会再发动攻击的可信保证。

他们表示，只有在这些保证到位后，伊朗才会准备就其核计划进行详细谈判。

以色列被广泛认为拥有原子武器库，但从未确认或否认拥有核武器，数十年来一直对这一问题保持所谓的模糊政策。

战前，伊朗曾表示愿意将60%浓缩铀库存的一半运出，这一水平远高于民用所需。

但消息人士称，在特朗普多次威胁打击伊朗后，这一立场发生了变化。

以色列官员告诉路透社，目前尚不清楚特朗普是否会决定发动攻击，以及他是否会给以色列开绿灯恢复军事行动。德黑兰誓言，如果遭到攻击将予以毁灭性回击。

然而，消息人士称，存在“可行的方案”来解决这一问题。

其中一名伊朗消息人士表示：“有一些解决方案，比如在国际原子能机构监督下稀释库存。”

国际原子能机构估计，当以色列和美国在2025年6月攻击伊朗核设施时，伊朗拥有440.9公斤60%浓缩铀。目前尚不清楚其中有多少得以保存。

国际原子能机构总干事格罗西3月表示，剩余库存“主要”存放在伊朗伊斯法罕核设施的隧道综合体中，该机构认为其中略多于200公斤的铀在那里。国际原子能机构还认为，部分铀存放在位于纳坦兹的大型核综合设施，伊朗曾在那里拥有两座浓缩设施。

伊朗表示，部分高浓缩铀用于医疗目的，以及德黑兰一座使用约20%浓缩铀的研究反应堆。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI, May 21 (Reuters) - Iran’s Supreme Leader has issued a directive that the country’s near-weapons-grade uranium should not be sent abroad, two senior Iranian sources said, hardening Tehran’s stance on one of the main U.S. demands at peace talks.

Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei’s order could further frustrate U.S. President Donald Trump and complicate talks on ending the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

Advertisement

Trump vowed on Thursday that the United States will not allow Iran to have its stockpile of highly enriched uranium.

“We will get it. We don’t need it, we don’t want it. We’ll probably destroy it after we get it, but we’re not going to let them have it,” Trump told reporters at the White House.

Israeli officials have told Reuters that Trump has assured Israel that Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium, needed to make an atomic weapon, will be sent out of Iran and that any peace deal must include a clause on this.

Advertisement

Israel, the United States and other Western states have long accused Iran of seeking nuclear weapons, including pointing to its move to enrich uranium to 60%, far higher than needed for civilian uses and closer to the 90% needed for a weapon. Iran denies seeking nuclear weapons.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has said he will not consider the war over until enriched uranium is removed from Iran, Tehran ends its support for proxy militias, and its ballistic missile capabilities are eliminated.

“The Supreme Leader’s directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country,” said one of the two Iranian sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity because of the sensitivity of the matter.

Advertisement

Iran’s top officials, the sources said, believe that sending the material abroad would leave the country more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel. Khamenei has the last say on the most important state matters.

When asked for comment for this story, White House spokeswoman Olivia Wales said : “President Trump has been clear about the United States’ red lines and will only make a deal that puts the American people first.”

Advertisement

Iran’s foreign ministry did not respond to request for comment.

Deep Suspicion Among Top Iranian Officials

A shaky ceasefire is in place in the war that began with U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28, after which Iran fired at Gulf states hosting U.S. military bases and fighting broke out between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

But there has been no big breakthrough in peace efforts, with a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports and Tehran’s grip on the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global oil supply route, complicating negotiations mediated by Pakistan.

The two senior Iranian sources said there was deep suspicion in Iran that the pause in hostilities was a tactical deception by Washington to create a sense of security before it renews airstrikes.

Advertisement

Iran’s top peace negotiator, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, said on Wednesday that “obvious and hidden moves by the enemy” showed the Americans were preparing new attacks.

Trump said on Wednesday the U.S. was ready to proceed with further attacks on Tehran if Iran did not agree to a peace deal, but suggested Washington could wait a few days to “get the right answers.”

The two sides have started to narrow some gaps, the sources said, but deeper splits remain over Tehran’s nuclear programme — including the fate of its enriched uranium stockpiles and Tehran’s demand for recognition of its right to enrichment.

Advertisement

Iran Hardens Stance On Enriched Uranium Stockpile

Iranian officials have repeatedly said Tehran’s priority is to secure a permanent end to the war and credible guarantees that the U.S. and Israel will not launch further attacks.

Only after such assurances are in place, they said, would Iran be prepared to engage in detailed negotiations over its nuclear program.

Advertisement

Israel is widely believed to have an atomic arsenal but has never confirmed or denied it has nuclear weapons, maintaining a so-called policy of ambiguity on the issue for decades.

Before the war, Iran signalled willingness to ship out half of its stockpile of uranium which has been enriched to 60%, a level far higher than what is needed for civilian uses.

But sources said that position changed after repeated threats from Trump to strike Iran.

Israeli officials have told Reuters it is still unclear whether Trump will decide to attack and whether he would give Israel a green light to resume operations. Tehran has vowed a crushing response if attacked.

Advertisement

However, the source said there were “feasible formulas” to resolve the matter.

“There are solutions like diluting the stockpile under the supervision of the International Atomic Energy Agency,” one of the Iranian sources said.

The IAEA estimates that Iran had 440.9 kg of uranium enriched to 60% when Israel and the U.S. attacked Iranian nuclear facilities in June 2025. How much of that has survived is unclear.

IAEA chief Rafael Grossi said in March that what remained of that stock was “mainly” stored in a tunnel complex in Iran’s Isfahan nuclear facility, and that his agency believed slightly more than 200 kg of it was there. The IAEA also believes some is at the sprawling nuclear complex at Natanz, where Iran had two enrichment plants.

Advertisement

Iran says some highly enriched uranium is needed for medical purposes and for a research reactor in Tehran which runs on relatively small amounts of uranium enriched to around 20%.

Related

Iran

Trump administration

Iran war

Enriched uranium

'That Seems To Be A Pattern': Obama Nails Trump's Short-Sightedness With Iran Nuke Deal
Iran's Top Diplomat Says A Lack Of Trust Is Impeding Talks To End War With The U.S.
U.S. Reportedly Considering Special Operation To Seize Iran’s Uranium
Senate Advances Bill Aimed At Ending Iran War As Cassidy, After Primary Loss, Flips To Support It

articles.title: Documents Show Queen Elizabeth Was 'Very Keen' For Ex-Prince Andrew To Become Trade Envoy
articles.content:
伦敦（美联社）——文件显示，已故女王伊丽莎白二世“非常希望”前安德鲁王子担任英国贸易特使。

英国政府于周四公布了与此项任命相关的机密文件，就在数月前，议员们指责这位国王的弟弟将对杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的友谊置于国家利益之上。

英国贸易机构负责人在一封信中写道：“女王非常希望约克公爵能承担起促进国家利益的重要角色。”

这位已故女王的介入将证实此前外界的猜测，即女王对儿子怀有软心肠——这种偏袒可能影响了她在处理有关安德鲁与爱泼斯坦关系的指控时缺乏决断。

今年2月，议员们通过了一项动议，要求公布这些文件。此前，这位曾为王子的安德鲁·蒙巴顿-温莎因涉嫌在担任贸易特使期间向爱泼斯坦分享政府报告而被逮捕。

贸易部长克里斯·布莱恩特在对议员的书面声明中表示，“我们没有发现任何证据表明在任命安德鲁担任此职务之前进行了正式的尽职调查或审查。”

“也没有证据表明曾考虑过这一点。这可以理解，因为这一新任命是王室参与贸易和投资推广工作的延续，此前肯特公爵决定辞去海外贸易委员会副主席职务，”他说。

他表示，政府正在配合泰晤士河谷警方对蒙巴顿-温莎可能存在的公职人员不当行为进行调查。

去年，蒙巴顿-温莎被剥夺了王室头衔，当时国王查理三世试图让君主制远离爱泼斯坦丑闻日益扩大的影响。这位前王子于2001年至2011年间担任国际贸易特使，后因对其与利比亚和阿塞拜疆可疑人物的关联担忧而被迫放弃该职位。

此前，美国司法部公布了数百万页与爱泼斯坦相关的文件。这些文件揭示了这位富有的金融家如何利用由富有权势人士组成的国际关系网来获取影响力，并对年轻女性和女孩进行性剥削。

文件公开后在英国引起的反响最为强烈，这场丑闻引发了人们对贵族、高级政客和有影响力的商人——即所谓“建制派”——如何运用权力的质疑。

蒙巴顿-温莎强烈否认任何不当行为。

LONDON (AP) — Documents show Queen Elizabeth II was “very keen” that the former Prince Andrew be given the job of UK trade envoy.

The U.K. government on Thursday released the confidential papers related to the appointment, just months after lawmakers accused the king’s brother of putting his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein ahead of the nation.

Advertisement

“The Queen is very keen that the Duke of York should take on a prominent role in the promotion of national interests,” the head of Britain’s trade body wrote in a letter.

The involvement of the late queen will confirm previously held beliefs that the monarch held a soft spot for her son — an empathy that might have influenced her lack of decisiveness in dealing with allegations of Andrew’s connection to Epstein.

Lawmakers approved a motion in February demanding publication of the documents after the one-time prince, now known simply as Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, was arrested on charges related to allegations that he shared government reports with Epstein while he was trade envoy.

Advertisement

Trade Minister Chris Bryant said in a written statement to lawmakers that “we have found no evidence that a formal due diligence or vetting process was undertaken” before Andrew was appointed to the role.

“There is also no evidence that this was considered. This is understandable since this new appointment was a continuation of the royal family’s involvement in trade and investment promotion work following the Duke of Kent’s decision to relinquish his duties as Vice-Chairman of the Overseas Trade Board,” he said.

He said that the government was cooperating with Thames Valley Police on their investigation into Mountbatten-Windsor and possible misconduct in public office.

Advertisement

Mountbatten-Windsor was stripped of his royal title last year as King Charles III tried to insulate the monarchy from the growing fallout from the Epstein scandal. The former prince served as a special envoy for international trade from 2001 to 2011, when he was forced to give up the role because of concerns about his links to questionable figures in Libya and Azerbaijan.

The move followed the U.S. Justice Department’s release of millions of pages of documents related to Epstein. Those files showed how the wealthy financier used an international web of rich, powerful friends to gain influence and sexually exploit young women and girls.

Advertisement

Nowhere has the fallout from the document release been felt more strongly than in the U.K., where the scandal has raised questions about the way power is wielded by the aristocracy, senior politicians and influential business owners, known collectively as “the Establishment.”

Mountbatten-Windsor has vehemently denied any wrongdoing.

Related

royal family

king charles

britain

prince andrew

British Royals Draw Further Back From Andrew As More Allegations Against Ex-Prince Emerge
Former Prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor Arrested
House Summons Former Prince Andrew To Testify About Epstein
